pi_items.source: NCBI/StatPearls - Septic Shock clinical presentation requires tachycardia with heart rate >90 bpm. Value of 115 bpm is consistent with compensated septic shock (warm shock) with elevated heart rate to compensate for reduced stroke volume.
Task.json metadata confirmed. In context of diastolic 45 mmHg, creates MAP of 57.33 mmHg, which is below the 65 mmHg threshold required for septic shock diagnosis per NCBI/StatPearls.
Task.json metadata confirmed. Consistent with severe hypotension seen in septic shock. Combined with systolic 82 mmHg yields MAP 57.33 mmHg (<65 mmHg threshold).
NCBI/StatPearls - Sepsis diagnostic criteria include fever >38°C or hypothermia <36°C. 39.2°C (102.56°F) represents moderate fever consistent with septic shock presentation.

Standard sepsis treatment guideline: Surviving Sepsis Campaign recommends rapid crystalloid IV fluid administration at ~30 mL/kg in first 3 hours. For 70kg patient = 700 mL/3hr ≈ 233 mL/hr. Value of 250 mL/hr is appropriate for sepsis fluid resuscitation.
Task.json confirmed. Tolerance of ±5 bpm is appropriate for medical simulation parameter tuning.
Task.json confirmed. Tolerance of ±5 mmHg is standard in medical device simulation for blood pressure parameters.
Task.json confirmed. Tolerance of ±0.5°C is appropriate for temperature measurement precision in medical monitors.
Task.json confirmed. Tolerance of ±5 mL/hr is appropriate for infusion pump flow rate precision.
Task.json metadata and setup_task.sh line 10, export_result.sh line 11: Confirmed screenshot file path for evidence collection.
Task.json metadata and setup_task.sh line 11, export_result.sh line 12: Confirmed JSON configuration report file path.

openice.info, MD PnP Program documentation: OpenICE is a real, peer-reviewed open-source medical device interoperability platform. It is a community implementation of ASTM F2761 standard. Available at https://www.openice.info/

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE supports simulated Multiparameter Monitor (MULTI), Infusion Pump, and other medical devices
pi_items.source: openice.info documentation confirms OpenICE includes medical device simulators for multiparameter monitors and infusion pumps for testing and development.
openice.info confirms Vital Signs is a demonstration app in OpenICE Supervisor that visualizes vital signs data from connected medical devices.

pi_items.verified_value: Septic shock is a life-threatening condition with systemic inflammation from infection leading to tissue hypoperfusion and organ dysfunction.
pi_items.source: NCBI/StatPearls - Septic Shock article (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK430939/) confirms septic shock as clinical syndrome requiring tachycardia, hypotension (requiring vasopressor support to maintain MAP≥65), and fever or hypothermia.

pi_items.verified_value: All vital sign parameters match established septic shock diagnostic criteria: HR 115 (>90 required), MAP 57.33 (<65 required), Temp 39.2°C (>38°C required)
pi_items.source: Verified through calculation: Systolic 82 + 2×Diastolic 45 / 3 = 57.33 mmHg, confirming hypotension threshold; confirmed against NCBI clinical literature.

privileged_info_summary: The sepsis simulation task targets clinically accurate vital signs for septic shock: heart rate 115 bpm (tachycardia >90 required), blood pressure 82/45 mmHg (MAP 57.33 mmHg, below 65 mmHg septic shock threshold), temperature 39.2°C (fever >38°C required), and infusion rate 250 mL/hr (standard sepsis fluid resuscitation per Surviving Sepsis Campaign). All parameters match diagnostic criteria from NCBI/StatPearls medical literature. The task uses OpenICE, a peer-reviewed open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital Harvard, which generates synthetic simulated patient data. The required deliverables are a screenshot of the Vital Signs app showing configured parameters and a JSON configuration report, both saved to /home/ga/Desktop/.
